steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what role the Algeria sentence plays in the passage. The Algeria sentence describes a shift away from solid fuels. The text then introduces Heltberg's Ghana study to show the energy ladder model is reductive — fuel choice depends on more than income.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It describes a trend that the text goes on to suggest has a similar cause as a seemingly unrelated trend observed in Ghana.': Claims Algeria and Ghana share the same cause. The passage never identifies a shared cause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It illustrates the kind of phenomenon that the text goes on to suggest is frequently but inadequately accounted for by the energy ladder.': Algeria shows a real-world fuel-switching trend that the energy ladder model is then said to handle poorly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It provides an example of a type of change that the text goes on to suggest is poorly suited for evaluating whether the energy ladder is a viable model.': Reframes Algeria as an evaluation of the model, but the passage uses Algeria as an example, not a test. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It introduces a finding that the text goes on to suggest can be explained in two different ways that are equally compelling.': Only one explanation (the ladder) is called inadequate; no second explanation is offered. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
